text: LOVED this movie. And I didn't even think the 2014 one was that great. They really addressed all the problems the first one had and so much more. Hell, this might even be my favorite TMNT movie. It was a blast to watch from start to finish, and I loved how the Turtles actually get some character development this time. Bebop and Rocksteady were extremely memorable and well-cast, and there's tons more epic setpieces than the first. It's pretty much the TMNT film I've waited decades for. It dropped the semi-serious tone for an all-out cartoon feel, and the movie is all the better for it. I really hope this film does well.

text: ALERT: Ranting teen.For all the people saying, "Only people with small IQ's and bad taste would enjoy this movie", do not understand or appreciate how much effort was put into this and the last TMNT movie. Just TRY to make a 3D animation and tell me how long it takes to make ten frames (less than a second of video). How come some of you even complain about "horrible" CGI, when that's as real of TMNT you could have them be? Yes I understand the flaws of the movie like how "they learned how to fight off of a book", but does having a rat learning how to fight from watching another man practice make it any more "realistic"? And why do you people complain about the "realism" of the movie and all its plot holes when they are trying to produce a movie about talking, gigantic, mutant ninja turtles?! Why do you people take this light-hearted, fun-filled movie like a spear into your heart? If it "ruined" your childhood, then why don't you go off and make a better movie. I've seen the old shows, movies, etc. I've also re-watched them after TMNT 2014 came out. And if you thought TMNT 2014 wasn't a LEAST bit better than the old MOVIES (acting, effects, plot, etc.), then you need to get the nostalgia out of your system. And the movie is directed towards 12 year olds... Lighten up.P.S. Just close your eyes and bring headphones when Megan Fox is on screen.

I hope Casey Jones isn't as annoying as he is in the current cartoon. Most of the time I just want to slap the shit out of him there, and the only time I ever liked him in the 2003 series was that time he tried to distract the Utroms by pretending to be an angry customer with a toaster that had RITC written on it in crayon, and even then I was more amused by the stone-faced expression the guy at the front desk's face while calling Casey out on that.
We're, he looks like the cliché "bad boy" with a buzz cut that got old about eight action movies ago. Personally, I think it would have been more interesting if Casey was at least black, Hispanic, or Italian-American rather than just another white guy for a white girl.

text:
I swear...if you gave me the budget this movie had...I would have made a TMNT movie SO GOOD....that it would be like The Dark Knight of Turtle movies. 
1) they already fucked up with the first one (i mean the first reboot one)
2) they are still ugly as sin
3) they made Beebop and Rocksteady so goofy and fake looking that....well I still have to see them but WHY!!!??? Why couldnt they have made some epic looking puppet costumes if the orignal movies did it? and did it WELL!
4) the original comic books were actually kind of gritty, fun but gritty and violent. Not overly violent, but realistic violence. 
5) if you made a PG-13 TMNT (considering the last one..the reboot was the only PG-13 one and yet not even close to as epic or gritty as the original movies) then WHY can't you make it BETTER...I mean you have PG-13 in your rating, you can get away with A LOT these days (sadly)...but what did they get away with? NINJA TURTLES HANGING ON A Victoria Secret BILLBOARD DURING THE CREDITS...like a bunch of horny turtles hanging on a pair of tits. YEAH real funny....more like creepy. 
6) they FUCKED UP SHREDDER.....how do you fuck up such a cool character? It is HARD to fuck up such a menacing COOL villain...yet they somehow managed to...like REALLY bad.
7) I still can't get over Shredder, what a WASTE of a character
8) watch the intro of Shredder in the original movie...watch how PERFECT he was introduced, how REAL he was...how COOL it is filmed (with style, and effort)....in this it is just like "HERE IS CGI SHREDDER...TRANSFORMERS-SHREDDER" oh....great.
9) FUCK HOLLYWOOD, they can't get shit right. Seriously STOP fucking up EVERY SINGLE FRANCHISE EVER KNOWN. HUNDREDS OF MILLIONS of dollars spent on....ugly turtles and cgi. FUCK YOU HOLLYWOOD.
author: @mhaze210

text: Geez... Is this the best they can do with the visual effects? I don't want to rant but Bebop and Rocksteady look like video game models. Seriously..look at them. They don't look real at all. The lighting does not match up with the surrounding light sources, making them look surreal. The movements are way too unnatural looking. It's awkward watching them in a real setting with real people.  They don't mix in well at all.  Wow.  Not only that, their lips seem to not sync up well with their dialogue. Isn't CGI supposed to eliminate that issue? And my God... enough with the TEAL and ORANGE filter.  It makes movies look depressing or overly saturated. Hollywood can't seem to shake that super obnoxious filter off any of their new films. It's like an amateur photoshop artist who keeps adding lens flares on all his pictures. Enough already.  Use sparingly!  -End Rant. lol

text:
Oh wow, are some of these comments silly. Personally think this one looks really good, even Megan and Will's one liners made me laugh. Although the first one wasn't the best, it had moments of awesome (though they were few and far between) so the potential is there. All they need to do is drive it closer to the cartoon! Maybe not make it so gritty, lighten it up, and maaaaybe focus less on Megan. She acted fine in the last one, but I didn't pay to see her. lol If they keep her jokes funny, make her less central then we're all good! I can already tell the turtles look brighter, and there's actual stuff that comes out of the darn cartoon!

Like, holy shit, actual foot clan! Casey Jones! FREAKIN' BEEBOP AND ROCKSTEADY! Plus I love It's Tricky, great song choice for the trailer. The last thing I hope for is the actual TMNT theme to be in the darn film! Staying optimistic, this one is off on good stead.

text: Now this is what I'm talking about, sure, the CGI looks  fucking atrocious, and so do the designs of the turtles, but Bebop and Rocksteady look good (as far as bad CGI is concerned). Not only that, but this trailer suggests that this next movie will be MUCH sillier than the 2014 movie. The 2014 Turtles movie had its silly moments, but this one seems like this next movie will be going all the way - the cheesy music, the more wisecracking turtles, the return of Bebop and Rocksteady, the return of the always wisecracking Casey Jones. Nothing here suggests a serious tone, and honestly, I like it that way; Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles is a stupid idea, why would you try to take something with a title like that seriously? You can have more serious and dark moments in this, but a much more fun and light-hearted tone seems to fit TMNT a lot better.

text: Let me just say this. I grew up with the turtles - original cartoon and the original movies. They were a part of my childhood and I will always love them. Now I am in my 30s, and my 6-year-old daughter loves them too. Its something we get to relate together on. We watch the old cartoons and movies on DVD and the new cartoon on Hulu. She loves the new cartoon best, and I can see why - it's silly, fun, adventurous, stylistic, full of character development, and has tons and tons of awesome fight scenes and action beats. But one thing she hates: the last TMNT (2014) movie, and again I can see why. The turtles are ugly, there are barely any martial arts, and the jokes are not funny. I am tired of people defending this atrocity by saying "you don't get it, it's for kids" - you are missing the point. If this new one is as careless as the last one, you can bet she will hate it as much as I will, and we will not be watching it together like we do all the other turtles stuff.  There, I said my piece; you can continue getting a boner over the totally lame casey jones, or fake looking bebop or whatever…

text: I want to like this movie. I really do. But, it's just so much I dislike! I mean, I appreciate that they are trying to mingle a bit of the 80s with a more trendy vibe. But some of it just doesn't work. From Gary Anthony Williams and Sheamus posing that the bumbling street thugs Bebop and Rocksteady. Or Tyler Perry being his most eccentric version of Baxter Stockman. It's all a little loony. And I am curious as to how much character development will go into that. Speaking of character development, I see Megan Fox has improved a little with seemingly a lot less dull. Why the hell couldn't we replace her and start from scratch is beyond me. But, I guess we need somebody that was more visually appealing. Also, Stephen Amell as Casey Jones. Wha--how the hell--who the hell thought of this? Granted, I like Arrow and Amell is a pretty cool guy but he's no Casey Jones. Think back to Elias Koteas and see where you fucked up at. His version of Casey Jones fits so well in the dynamic of these Turtles. But, maybe they see something I don't see. Overall, I am a little on the fence with this. I might as well say fuck it because I will most likely watch it anyway. Making this entire rant pointless. However, I most certain that it wouldn't change a how I feel about the film.

text:
I really don't know what to say... Well, I guess it looks better than the previous film which I couldn't even watch for more than 30 minutes before I just had to stop but that's all I can say. Not a bad trailer though. The awful remix of a catchy and popular 80's song is perfectly fitting for this modern TMNT rehash!

BTW what is up with sexualising April so much. Who are these films made for? The adults who grew up with the old turtles or the kids who love the new cartoons? If it was the adults then the changes to Turtles seem weird but if it's the kids then the over reliance on sex seems weird. They should really pick their audience better. Also Megan Fox "playing" April again... ugh, she can't act for shit! Couldn't they just replace her with an actual actress? If they don't want an actual actress then why don't they go with a blowup doll. It could emote at least as well as Megan Fox!

Hey, they brought in Casey Jones (portrayed by Steven Amell of "Arrow" series), Baxter Stockman (Tyler Perry? Hmm, okay), Bebop and Rocksteady, even a REAL fricking Foot Clan!

Even the design for the Turtles has changed a bit--less of a "salvaged from scraps" theme and more of a battle-ready look.

Except for the continually annoying presence of Megan Fox, I think I'm actually interested in where this movie is headed! Maybe I'll just have to forget the first one existed.

text:
I'm a TF fan...and after watching this trailer...Michael Bay....all is forgiven!

What more could a TMNT fan want in a turtles movie;

1. Allusion to dimension X, a potential Krang set up in this movie or the inevitable sequel!
2. The party Wagon....firing manhole covers!!
3. Rocksteady and Bepop in their mutant and human forms dressed in their iconic attire!
4. Casey Jones played by none other than Stephen Amell from Arrow!
5. Baxter Stockman in his original comic incarnation!?
6. Foot soldiers- real human ninjas!

I mean what more could a fan want...I mean really. 

Honestly I'm looking forward to this more than Batman/Justice league movie. At least I'll be going into this with low expectations so if it's bad I won't be disappointed. 

But Jesus, Rocksteady and Bepop, what more could a guy who grew up with the original series want?

text: If there is one thing Bay is known for, that is fanservice. In fact, his whole films are like candy like make them look good, but don't actually taste good, not that I'm complaining. As much as I hate to admit it, I'm actually looking forward to this film as oppose to the first one, which I thought was really, really weak. I don't expect it to be good personally or even a masterpiece, but I'm sure it will be able to satisfy any Turtles fan like myself who grow up with them. And it is nice that they are bringing even more characters in from the turtles universe. Also I would say Stephan Amell is an odd choice for Casey Jones, but since Arrow, I'm looking forward to see how he plays out, since he's no stranger to comic book vigilante heroes. Still, I'm just going to take it for what it is worth and not be some crybaby fanboy about it.

text: One of my biggest gripes is the overuse of CGI in just about all entertainment today. I'm sorry, but in THIS instance, and in MY opinion, CGI fails to deliver an impressive and convincing array of characters. I swear, and I bet a lot of people my age (34) would agree that the former make up pf the characters i.e., ACTUAL stitched costumes combined with animatronics. There is no argument that the Ninja Turtles from the 1st movie in 1990 looked FAR better AND convincingly real when compared with this CGI shit-show that encompasses practically all of the new generation. Were I more computer savvy, I would love to put together a small video showing the non-comparison of the old versus the new turtles and subsequent characters. I know people my age who remember the 1st TMNT movie will tell you; those WERE the Ninja Turtles. For all intents and purposes, the Ninja Turtles were real, and I distinctly remember my cousin and I talking all the time about how we were going to meet them once we got old enough to purchase, rent or borrow a small convertible and make our way to Jim Hensens Creature Shop to meet the Turtles face to face!

text: Ok, the first move wasn't great, but this movie has headed into the direction everybody wanted. We've got Bebop and Rocksteady. We've got a new villain with Shredder blades that doesn't look like a terribly CGI Transformer. We've got a fucking Oliver Queen Casey Jones! (Favorite part BTW). We've got a cool dumptruck for the turtles. I guess that's cool. And I'm really happy that it looks like there is some real danger coming to the city and not some CGI Transformer trying to spread a virus so he can cure it to get money and power. I will gladly get hyped over this one, because this one is becoming less what Michael Bay wants and more what we've always wanted. I'm proud of him.

text: Ehhhh a few things. As a lover of films and TMNT I did watch the first one. On DVD not in theaters. It was bad. The turtles and Shredder looked stupid, the jokes were not funny, and the plot was generic. Now it looks like Bay has strapped into to make the same movie and ruin two more beloved TMNT villains. The only redeeming quality I can see in this is Casey Jones because they cast a good actor. And sure you can argue that it's like this because TMNT is for kids, fine, but look at Pixar, the best kids movies are ones that kids and adults can enjoy on separate levels. Anyways, this is just a trailer and it's impossible to judge movies based off trailers. If people did that no one would go see movies. So I'll be optimistic and hope it's good, but I'm definitely bit spending money to see it in theaters.

My name is Oliver Queen. For five years I was "stranded" on an island with only one goal... survive. Now I will fulfill my father's dying wish... to use a list of names he left me, to bring down those who are poisoning my city. To do this, I must become someone else. I must become something else

text: WAIT JUST A MINUTE, "Out of the Shadows"? They named it after the video game based on the 2012/current TV series that was first released in 2013? That's what I would've preferred the first movie to have been about. I thought they were going to just simply name it "Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles 2". And the Turtles are going to reveal themselves to the public and Baxter Stockman and Bebop and Rocksteady are going to make their very first big screen appearance? That just blew my mind away. I wish my dad was still around to see this. He liked Bebop and Rocksteady. And the Turtles' newest vehicle at 0:26 looks better than that van they had shown at the end of the first movie. It reminds me of the hauler vehicle that was first shown of the Back To The Sewer episodes of season 7 of the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles 2003-2009 animated series http://farm4.staticflickr.com/3014/2873375994_731362205a.jpg. I would've preferred a realistic version of the shellraiser vehicle of the 2012/current TV series and I would've also preferred doing the Triceraton invasion of the sequel just like they did of the last episode of season 3 of the 2012/current TV series.

text:
Honestly man why do they keep letting Bay make movies, he literally has one style that doesn't apply to everything and this title would be one of those things.  I really like the Turtles, I want them to get a proper movie but what Bay is doing just doesn't seem like a good direction to me.  It will probably do okay at the box office just b/c many people have a soft spot for the turtles, for me they sparked my love in art and drawing.  In third grade I saw a drawing my best friend did of a ninja turtle and I wanted to be able to do that so I got to practicing and that turned it into a life long hobby.  I'm no professional artist mind you but it's a hobby I enjoy and that has brought me joy over the years and it started with Ninja Turtles.

I'm sure I'll watch this movie, as I'm one of the people that have a fondness for the Turtles but I really wish they would go a different way with the franchise.

text: I've been a turtles fan since the 1980's. I have mixed feelings about this. The special effects are amazing (though I question why the turtles are so large. They're not supposed to be so big). Megan Fox is hot (albeit not that bright). And it's nice for the turtles to fight a villain besides Shredder. Still, I think that the movie is trying to be too "contemporary". text: Will this make money? Certainly. Is it going to be good? Definitely not. I get this is going to be a kids movie but this is as much for the parents as it for the kids. They're the ones who originally grew up with it. There needs to be something good for them. It's one thing to do a nod to the source material, it's another to force it just so it's there. They did the same thing with the first Transformers movie and it came off cheesy. Keep the spirit of the source material and make a decent movie from it; don't force it. Directors, producers, and studios need to understand this. So far the only thing that looks decent are the turtles themselves, and Bebop and Rocksteady. As for Amell being cast in this role, honestly Casey Jones is just too similar to Oliver Queen for my liking. Would have loved to see him do something not so close to his Arrow role.

text: Wow blows my mind hearing adults posting videos about their negative reactions towards this movie not living up to their "adult" expectations when it is targeted towards kids. Of course haters also justify their negativity about it being based on all the hate Michael Bay gets. Its not meant to have realism in it or award winning plots, actors or special effects, it is meant to be fun and cartoony for kids and Nickelodeon a kids channel franchise has the rights to it. If you want to complain about a movie being too cartoony try picking a movie not based around mutant turtles who are ninja's and spend their days eating pizza. If you look back on the comics they were way different than the cartoons or any movies involving ninja turtles this movie franchise is based on the cartoon universe of ninja turtles. I can't wait to see it and Bebop and Rocksteady look awesome, glad they brought back Bebop's classic glasses. Him and DC comics version Captain Cold share similar taste :P

text:
YES! I'm in, saw the first, it was ok, but I was really disappointed. The turtles were WAAAY too big and an ugly design, they were hulking, also too much Megan Fox, wrong casting for April, I would have cast Olivia Trilby (with her exact look from Dredd), and Splinter should have been WAAAY more badass,  more Shredder, liked his design, less of Will Arnett, more Baxter Stockman (who they dropped part 2 for fn Madea, for no reason, WTF!!!).

Now on to what was good about this trailer, pretty much everything, Spoilers, nope, they just showed us the good stuff that we wanted to see, and not too much of it. Rocksteady and Bebop looked AWESOME, the additions seem really cool, but Casey Jones should have had long hair for sure (minor gripe), hey let's do no fn work at all to change his look, also "Brundle fly" is going to be gross looking and cool, (just a guess on my part).

I'm actually exited for this movie!!:) I think I'm speaking for a lot of people here, old school (like myself), and new. Cowabunga!! and Booyakasha!! Peace;)

text: two things...first, the first TMNT was actually a fun kids movie. I didn't mind it, hell I even thought Megan Fox did a okay job. second. they brought back two of my childhood favorites, Bebop and Rocksteady. I'm also wondering if Krang is going to show up since they are doing a whole alien invasion thing. that would be cool to see. my only grip is that Micheal Bay is making it. the last movie was fine, but then again so was the first Transformers. after the first one it was a never ending decent into mediocrity that made the original look like a cinematic masterpiece and that wont end for at least another three sequels as Bay is already set to Direct at least one more Transformers film. hate being negative with movies but history tells me this is going to suck. I hope it doesn't and Bay does a good job.

text:
Well I'll be dipped in shit!  I cant believe Michael Bay actually listened to the fans!

Beebop and Rocksteady!  And Casey Jones!!!

I think I'm gonna have to quit bitching about Bay for awhile...that kinda hurts, but this trailer is cool enough that I just have to.  :)

I swore up and down that I wouldn't ever watch the first one.  Then I watched the Honest Trailer, and where the little kid comes on at the end and talks about it being a kids movie and to just have fun...that's when I got over myself, rented it, turned my critical brain off for a couple hours, and just enjoyed the shit out of myself.  :)

I'll definitely be seeing this in theaters.  Cant wait!

text:
Though I can't conclude a movie to be incompetent based off a trailer, I see no inspiration to this movie's marketing scheme. They use every cliche method of creating hype.

1. The dichotomy of loud, catchy beats with futuristic, bass noises.
2. Color contrast which is used to fabricate innovative picture quality.
3.  Flashy, eye-alluring CGI, which both ruins potential in spectacle of practical effects and stagnates creativity in its excessive use.
4. Hype-driven, sporadic pacing with a climactic conclusion seeks to excite the viewer and create the domino effect of informing friends of the trailer.
5. Using Megan Fox again from the previous movie as a sex object. I don't wanna start the argument of SJW's, but she's once again being objectified as a sexual image to gain viewership. I mean come on, she was gonna take her top off.

I could keep on going. But what I really wanna publicize is that people should skip on this one. Again, it's a trailer, but this allocates to the pattern of squandered creativity in the cinematic industry. We, as viewers, have the duty of supporting cinematography that innovates and broadens the horizons of artistic expressionism. Please guys, you've seen movies like this a thousand times over. We deserve better.

text: Kind of like you said. We have what we want and what we don't want. I'm 34, so I will judge everything TMNT till the end of time by the standard and precedent of the original Turtles I grew up with (the REAL TMNT, right?). So... why are the Turtles 6 and a half feet tall? They're supposed to be 5 ft. Why is Vernon a heterosexual sexual predator? He is supposed to be gay, aloof, and nerdy. Why isn't April looking natural and professional in that yellow suit? Instead she is looking like a 2014 pop star with a nose job. Why does Casey Jones look like a clueless high-schooler and not a bad ass 40-something sinister recluse? Shredder never keeps his gear off for long so why start now? Continued below...

text:
Hell yeah! Bebop and Rocksteady!
I don't care about people whining and moaning about about new movies, especially reboots. I watch to be distracted with a story,  and pretty much most movies ever made decent stories.
If your goal is to wander around look for things to bitch about, congrats, you're the reason we can't really have anything new or different. You have to have it, "the way things used to be" so much you've made a lot of people and industries afraid to change, advance, or innovate. They just pump out the same thing just slightly better or slightly different. The moment someone takes a big leap, most people are stuck in their ways. People constantly turning into the joke they made about their grandparents who stick with the older stuff they grew up watching and listening too. 

It's something different, something newish,
There's no such thing as a perfect movie, perfect series, perfect anything.. Except maybe Leeloo.. Multipass?

text: Compare this trailer to the original trailer for the first movie. The trailer for the first movie tried to make it look like a really serious action film, like it was desperate to be the next Batman Begins. This trailer makes this movie look like it's trying to capture the fun and zaniness of the 80's cartoon...and that's why I'm much more interested in this movie than I was the first one. And FINALLY we get Bebop and Rocksteady!

text: I'm gonna admit, I actually liked the first one. Not as in I thought it was great, fantastic, or a 10/10, but it entertained me a lot more than I thought it would. And although the CGI on Bebop and Rocksteady needs some work, it's actually looking better than the first movie. Right off the bat, the turtles look a lot better, they're brighter and have less stuff on them like last time, except for Donnie who I guess began watching Family Matters. Also, I believe the ships coming down are the Krang, and are from Dimension X. Shredder also ditched his transformer, for a more practical suit too. And finally, and this has been confirmed by the director, the brothers are the biggest part of this trailer, and will be for the movie. According to the director, the turtles will be the focal point, and throughout, they hope to not only show their more lighthearted and comedic side, but also emotional side too. If they can improve upon the first, it would be great to see.

text:
I can't stand how cliche this trailer is. The Inception-esque "BWAAAAHHH" music in the opening that gets replaced by obnoxious dubstep, the fading in and out from black, the music pausing at the punchlines.. 

Trailer aside, the movie looks okay aside from the Michael Bay-isms. Explosions everywhere and that one gratuitous shot of Megan Fox's chest at 1:53. Still, I'll probably have fun with it if it's got some decent action and the writing isn't cringe-inducing. The first one wasn't too bad so I'm open to it.

text:
TL;DR: Despite this movie's issues so far, it still looks like a ton of guilty fun, just like the last one was, and I'll still be happy to see it.

Extended: Well, first off, those Dimension X ships look pretty shitty, much like the manmade Decepticons were in Transformers 4, so I don't think I'll be looking forward to that. I can't believe they brought Will Arnett back for this one, since he clearly wasn't fucking funny at all in the first one, strangely enough. Further sexual exploitation of Megan Fox as April, as well as a bunch of familiar Bay-isms in the action (not to mention the general return of Michael Bay as the producer), are definite groaners. And most importantly of all, I have the most trouble with the fact that the turtles are actually coming out in the open for all of New York to see. That *NEVER* happens in any other iteration of the Ninja Turtles, and is completely uncharacteristic to their code of secrecy as ninja, to remain in the shadows, for the quite rational fear that humans would reject their mutant appearance. I'd be a lot angrier about this if I actually gave a shit, but we clearly know exactly how this movie is going to turn out, so I don't really feel anything at all towards it.

As for the positives, THANK GOD they didn't cast Shia LaBeoff as Casey Jones. Tyler Perry as Baxter Stockman is actually a surprisingly fitting choice, and Bebop and Rocksteady look legitimately badass, and definitely very 80's-esque, which'll give the G1 fans nostalgia boners for sure. Overall, this looks destined to become another dumbass-awesome guilty pleasure movie of mine, and I'm calmly looking forward to it when it comes out.

text: I have to say I can definitely feel the tone shift here. It's similar to the direction the Amazing Spider-Man series took. The first movie was all dark and edgy and what-not, and then the second one got lighter and more colorful. I can feel that here. The addition of Baxter Stockman, Bebop and Rocksteady (say your pwayers, Toitles!), the overall less bulky and more agile looking Turtles and Shredder, Casey Jones, possibly the Krang and the Technodrome. It's all here. Hopefully it doesn't flop, though, this looks miles ahead of what we got the first time and really hits that nostalgia (plus,  I worry for Stephen Amell, but Arrow will get him through if this movie ends up failing!).

text: Look I like Stephen Amell a lot on Arrow but him as Casey Jones just shows they could care less about the property. He is built wrong (not thin and tall) has short hair, wrong color hair, and does not embody that late 80s slacker tough guy attitude (Casey Jones was a late 80s Metal Head, hence his original comment on hating punkers). "but it's a different take" or "it's a reboot" or "It's his interpretation of". If you take out the character history of a character then you are left with and interchangeable guy who anyone can play and no one care who does or who he is, and that is the definition of a bad character development and bad writing.

text:
you know the thing i really love about all these movies? Not the plot.. not the characters, not the movie.. No what i love about it.. the fandom.
The people in this fandom are doing the same thing as the people in the starwars, transformers, avengers, justice league, or any other fandom. 
HALF of them really love it.
HALF of them really hate it.
ALL OF THEM will go see it.
Half so they can say how great it was.
Half so they can bitch about how bad it was.
BUT.. love or hate it.. YOU WILL go see it.
Don't deny it, 
don't pretend you wont.
Hate or love it.. you will be there EITHER REASON. To Scream about how great it is, or whine about how awful it is. YOU WILL go see it. 
That said.. its pretty cool looking.

text: Tbh, I really hated the first movie. I thought it was the most boring lazy script ever, and the turtles had no personality. Plus I hate when movies rehash the whole dark, melodramatic, destiny filled origin story bullshit. But this one looks much better. The turtles are already established and now they're actually taking advantage of the universe and the characters. We got fucking Casey Jones skating away from a charging Rocksteady, I mean that looks awesome. And might that vortex in the sky be a portal to Dimension X? I'm going to try not to get my hopes too high, but this looks like it could be dope

text: Is it really this difficult to understand the difference between a director and a producer?  I never once heard anybody consider "man of Steel" a Christopher Nolan movie, and guess what? He was a producer on it, but it's a Zack Snyder movie. You would never say Christopher Nolan fucked man of Steel up (if you didn't like it) Well, realize that Michael Bay is a PRODUCER on this movie, not writer, editor or director , and no it's not the same thing.  A producer isn't directly involved in making the movie at all , or directing it or writing it. A producer puts up a portion of the money for the movie to get filmed. Once it finshed and editing they give their opinions on what they liked and didn't like, but that's it. So this isn't a Michael Bay movie and neither was the first ninja turtles movie. They put Michael Bays name everywhere so it can sell tickets.  That's all

text:
When I saw Megan Fox in the trailers for the first movie, I rolled my eyes, and was kinda miffed, because she looks nothing like the original April from the cartoon, and was probably there for shoe-horned sex appeal.
I no longer have a problem with it. My reasons?
Well, first of all, if you're going to have April be sexy, thank you for not making her all baby-faced and look like a teenager, I will give these movies props for that.
Second: Stephen Amell is in this movie, and he is to women who like men what Megan Fox is to men who like women. And even if he at no point takes off his shirt (I can watch Arrow for that), it's gonna be damn sexy seeing him kick ass in this movie.
And if anyone gripes that April does nothing but get herself into trouble that she needs to be saved from, I will put my foot so far up their ass, they're tasting the band-aids on my knees (don't ask).

text:
Hey look, the Foot ninja actually look like ninja! Hey look, they're introducing characters from the 80's cartoon+ Casey Jones! Hey look, they're totally using the Kraang and the subtitle of a game based on the 2012 series, which is totally not going to confuse people who already played that game with this upcoming movie! Hey look, they're using old stuff AGAIN!

Great, because of the reception of the first movie, now this will be "Remember this? Now it's back!" and mostly nostalgia based. Don't get me wrong, it's nice to pay nods to the originals once in a while, but not ENTIRELY homaging the originals, and deviating too far is also bad (We know the response to the first movie). I know I'll get "Don't judge it until it comes out" comments, but until I see there is SOMETHING new, I'm not going to change my mind. Also, isn't it too early for the turtles to reveal themselves to the public?

text: Ok, I'll admit it. This looks kinda cool. It may end up being bad but this looks like it will be an enjoyable kind of bad. They've clearly made some adjustments to where the last movie failed. Shredder is no longer playing second fiddle to anyone, which is great since he's one of my favourite villains ever. It's also cool to see Bebop, Rocksteady and Stockman (who seems to be a mix of his comic counterpart and his 1987 cartoon counterpart). One gripe I have is Stephen Amell as Casey Jones. I love the guy on Arrow but he's really not that great of an actor. So I'm not sure if he can pull of the cocky wisecracking tough guy that Casey is. However, Stephen's amazing at doing stunt work so we'll at least see some kickass fighting from him. Also, that mask looks pretty badass.

text:
This sequel seems to be fixing all the mistakes the first movie made.

The Foot Clan actually LOOK and function like ninjas, Shredder's back in his true look (no Megatron copycat mech suit) and Beebop and Rocksteady actually appear promising, this movie's take on Casey Jones is much like the old movie's version and the Turtle Van looks pretty cool.

That being said, I still won't give Michael Bay's vision of the TMNT my full attention. He's never earned my respect

text:
"Alright, guys. We did it. We succesfully made a good trailer showcasing all the improvements we've made in the second one, while hyping up the young with great action and popular music, and the old with recognizable characters, and a sense of lighthearted fun and campiness intertwined with seriousness and action. And best of all, we managed to keep it hidden from Michael Bay. Now to just upload it and watch our hard work pay off. And... Done. Hold on... Is that... Is that a shot of Megan Fox in a schoolgirl outfit undressing inbetween shots of the turtles leaping from spaceship to spaceship and bebop and rocksteady on motorcycles? What the... Jerry, did you put it in there? Craig? How about you, Larry? NO, I DIDN'T PUT IT IN THERE! But if none of us put it in there, then who- 
BAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAY! [ ...AY... AY... AY...]"

text:
When the first one was written, they of went in the direction of the Peter Laird and Kevin Eastman version (80's comic) the graphic novel series were actually for more of a mature audience and good.(teenager and up). All red bandanas, killed the shredder in the series, killed hundreds and hundreds of foot soldiers. Casey was in it, got April pregnant, stockman was in it but he was a nut towards the end. The turtles actually travel across the galaxy and fight trisartons (humanoid versions of triceratops )
When you read first few chapters you actually notice them growing up. Leo and Ralph have that love-hate relationship and is a lot more angry. The stories go onto different series. "City at war." Was good. They have the series at Barnes and Noble. I think it's three or four volumes. I have volume 1 and 2.

text: Oh boy, am I pretty mixed about this trailer. And I've got a lot to say, so lets do Pros and Cons! Con- 0:18 Alien Space Ships attacking a large city, wow, haven't seen that a million times already! Pro- Seems like they're building up Krang, and it'll be awesome to see him in his live action debut. Con- If recent trailers are anything to go by, he'll most likely pop up in the next one along with 90% of the movie. Pro- 0:26 Tartaruga Brothers? Is that a nod to the 2003 Series? Pretty cool! Con- 1:13 THAT'S Casey Jones? I mean, he doesn't look awful, but he just kind of looks like a guy in cosplay! Pro- 1:48 Now THAT is more like it! Bebop and Rocksteady are here, and they actually look pretty good! Con- Out of the Shadows? Is that really what they're naming it, after the video game? I'm starting to think Half Shell was a better subtitle! There's a lot more to say about this movie, but I'll just have to go and see it, since it's got me that intrigued! I'm not expecting it to be good, but it's starting to look like it'll be better than the first one!

text:
Good trailer. I noticed that the turtles were shrunken down a bit compared to the first movie(I didn't have a problem with them being huge in the first film though, they're *mutants* after all), and the Foot Ninjas are.....well, ninjas, even though using guns in the first film made complete & total sense(I actually liked that modern day Foot Clan they were going with) , swords wouldn't do well against machine guns. Sure, swords *can* deflect bullets, they can cut bullets in half too, but humans cannot react quick enough to deflect every shot.

Its screwed up that the freakin' Producer, is mentioned first, instead of the damn Director. Bay didn't direct this or the first film, so why is he, the dude that almost ruined the first TMNT film with the plan to have the turtles be aliens, getting top billing? If anything, that would be a *bad* thing, that would make some people avoid the film, thinking that Bay directed it when he didn't(and it doesn't help that Megan Fox is in film, who shockingly does an ok April O'Neil performance, but if people judge by this trailer alone & never saw the first film, they'll think she's there just for eye candy).

text: This is so incredible! It looks faithful to the show, beebop and rocksteady look awesome here, and the most important thing of all is that it doesn't seem to take itself to seriously which was one of the things that makes the whole idea of teenage mutant ninja turtles work. The problem that I had with the last movie was that I didn't know what it was trying to do. Was it trying to be a serious turtles movie or a humorous one? its not like they can't mix  darker tone with a good sense of humour hell look at how good they did it in the original tmnt film. The first of the Michael bay turtle movies wasn't mixing a darker tone with humour very well and it suffered for it. but looking at this new trailer I have to admit that I am excited!

text:
So a turn off your brain action flick like all the TMNT movies but the first live action one?

I can dig it.  TV series is awesome if I want something closer to comic material(2003).  But for the sake of just fun, this looks like it could be a bit of fun and that's important considering how every other movie seems to be taking themselves seriously.  

This is very obviously a kids movie, which is what TMNT is unless they go the Dark Christopher Nolan route.   Nice to see this one isn't though as all movies seem to go that route.

If it's an entertaining action romp that can be thoroughly enjoyed most of the way through then it will have done it's job.

text: They're gonna be pumping these movies out too, eh? I'd expect nothing less, but this trailer came a bit sooner than expected. To me, a lot of the content seems pretty tacked on just for the sake of it. It looks very busy. We have the introduction of Casey, the creation of Bebop & Rocksteady, more backstory on Shredder it seems, we have what's probably Dimension X being introduced...all fitted into an hour and a half, more or less? The only way that can be done is just to be pushed along by bouts of action and per the action movie tradition, lots of explosions and destruction. I want to be excited for this film, but it really does seem to have that quick cash-in feel, even more than the first. Oh, and Casey Jones doesn't look NEARLY as rough and tumble as he should.

A normal shredder, the foot clan being ninjas instead of mercenaries, Bebop and Rockstead, Casey Jones, Baxter Stockman, It's all there.

-Bebop will be played by Uncle Ruckas
-Rocksteady will be played by that wrestler Sheamus
-Baxter Stockman will be played by Tyler Perry
-AND THE ONE THING I WANTED JUST GOT CONFIRMED! THEY SHRUNK THE HULK SIZED TURTLES DOWN! OH THANK GOD.
